text: Senior backend engineer with seven years of production experience. The last four years have been on payments and ledger systems at two fintech startups. Comfortable owning critical money-movement code end to end, from API design through audit-ready logging.

Currently a Senior Backend Engineer at Northwind Pay (Series A, ~80 employees, $400M annualized payment volume). Joined as employee number 22 in early 2024. I lead the ledger and reconciliation services and recently shipped a rewrite of the settlement pipeline.

Led the rewrite of Northwind's daily settlement pipeline from a Cron-driven Python script to a Temporal workflow with Postgres-backed idempotency. Reduced settlement-day errors by 90 percent and cut the close-of-books window from six hours to under one hour.

Designed and implemented Northwind's double-entry ledger from scratch. Used Postgres with serializable isolation for the entry-write path and an event-sourced read model for reporting. Authored the design document and got sign-off from compliance and the auditor.

Previously a Backend Engineer at Acme Issuing (acquired by Marqeta in 2022). Joined as a mid-level engineer and was promoted to senior in 18 months. Worked on the card authorization path and ISO 8583 message handling.

At Acme Issuing I owned the ISO 8583 parser and the authorization decision engine. Wrote and operated the integration with two card networks (Visa and Mastercard) and shipped a fraud-rules engine that reduced false declines by 12 percent in the first quarter.

Python: seven years of production experience. Comfortable with asyncio, type hints, modern Python tooling (uv, ruff, pyright), FastAPI, Pydantic, and SQLAlchemy. Have profiled and optimized hot paths in production.

Postgres: five years in production, including running it under non-trivial load. Have used logical replication for blue-green migrations, debugged lock contention with pg_stat_activity, and have strong opinions about when to use Postgres versus a queue.

Kafka: two years of production experience. Owned a Kafka cluster for the event spine at Northwind, wrote consumers in Python, and have debugged consumer lag, partition rebalances, and the joys of exactly-once semantics.

On-call: have been on a one-week-in-six rotation at both companies. At Northwind I am also the de facto incident commander for payment outages. I have a strong opinion that the goal of on-call is to keep the customer whole, not to be the hero.
BS in Computer Science from the University of Waterloo, 2017. Co-op rotations at Stripe and Shopify during undergrad gave early exposure to payments and high-traffic systems.
Side project: I maintain a small open-source library that implements double-entry accounting in pure Python with a Postgres adapter. About 800 stars on GitHub and used by a handful of small fintechs.
